pi_items.metadata_value: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), Subject S001
pi_items.verified_value: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0) - Real public dataset with Subject S001 recordings available
pi_items.source: https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/ - PhysioNet official dataset page confirms existence of 64-channel EEG recordings from 109 subjects including S001 with motor imagery tasks

pi_items.verified_value: Fp1=prefrontal left, Fp2=prefrontal right, C3=central motor left, C4=central motor right, P7=parietal left, P8=parietal right, O1=occipital left, O2=occipital right in 10-20/10-10 system
pi_items.source: https://www.emotiv.com/blogs/how-to/understanding-the-10-20-system-of-eeg-electrode-placement - Standard 10-20 EEG electrode nomenclature; confirmed odd numbers = left hemisphere, even = right hemisphere

pi_items.verified_value: C3 (channel 3) and C4 (channel 4) are the primary motor cortex electrodes - correct for motor imagery analysis
pi_items.source: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9774545/ - EEG Channel Selection Techniques in Motor Imagery Applications review confirms C3/C4 as essential sensorimotor region electrodes; https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7895585/ confirms ERD patterns specific to C3 and C4
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S2352340924001525 - Documents 8-30 Hz as the standard frequency band containing Mu (8-13 Hz) and Beta (13-30 Hz) for motor imagery
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S2352340924001525 - Documents 8-30 Hz as the standard motor imagery frequency band (Mu 8-13 Hz + Beta 13-30 Hz)

pi_items.verified_value: FFT Plot widget exists in OpenBCI GUI and displays frequencies and their amplitudes
pi_items.source: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/ - Official OpenBCI documentation confirms FFT Plot as an available data visualization widget

pi_items.verified_value: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 is a real release published August 21, 2023
pi_items.source: https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2 - Official GitHub release page confirms v5.2.2 exists with Ganglion decompression update

pi_items.verified_value: File is claimed to be derived from PhysioNet eegmmidb (64-channel) converted to 8-channel OpenBCI format
pi_items.source: Setup script references /workspace/data/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt; actual file content cannot be verified without environment runtime; channel reduction from 64 to 8 is documented as feasible in motor imagery research

pi_items.verified_value: Disable Fp1, Fp2, P7, P8, O1, O2 - consistent with motor imagery domain knowledge that non-motor-cortex channels add noise
pi_items.source: Domain knowledge from motor imagery research confirms prefrontal (Fp1/Fp2), parietal (P7/P8), and occipital (O1/O2) regions are not primary motor cortex and should be excluded for focused motor cortex analysis

privileged_info_summary: The motor imagery channel protocol task uses real data from the PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), Subject S001, converted to 8-channel OpenBCI format. The electrode configuration (Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2) follows standard 10-20 nomenclature. The task correctly identifies C3 and C4 as primary motor cortex electrodes and requires disabling all non-motor channels. The bandpass frequency range of 8-30 Hz correctly captures the Mu (8-13 Hz) and Beta (13-30 Hz) frequency bands essential for motor imagery analysis. The FFT Plot widget exists in OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023).
